zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Cheney II於1520年出生在英國艾可沃爾坦 艾比時，他的父親Robert Cheney Sr.31歲，母親Annis Harrison29歲。他於1550年1月4日之前與Joane Harrison在英國艾可沃爾坦 艾比結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1567年10月31日在英國艾可沃爾坦 艾比享年47歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭希迪 本 倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Cheney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Chaney   with the same etymology as in 2 below.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by or in an oak wood, from Old French chesnai ‘oak grove’ (from chesne ‘oak’), or a habitational name from any of several places called (Le) Cheney, e.g. in Haute-Savoie, named with this word. Compare Chesney .

French Canadian: altered form of French Chesnay or Chenay, topographic names with the same meaning and etymology as in 2 above, or habitational names from (Le) Chesnay or (Le) Chenay, names of several places in the northwestern part of France, based on Old French chesnai ‘oak grove’. Compare Chenette and Chenier 2.

variants.name: Chenette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Armand.
Altered form of French Canadian Chenet, itself an (intermediate) altered form of Cheney 3. Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.
History: The (majority of the) Chenettes are descendants of Bertrand Chesnay/Chenay dit/sieur de La Garenne from Brittany (France); see Cheney .

variants.name: Chaney
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any one of several places in Normandy, Picardy, or Artois (France) named (Le) Quesnay or Quesnoy, or elsewhere in northern France named (Le or La) Chesnay or Chesnoy. The placenames all derive from Old French chesnai or its Normanno-Picard equivalent quesnai ‘oak grove’ (medieval Latin casnetum).
French: variant of Chanet, a habitational name from any of various places called Chanet or Le Chanet, from Latin canna ‘reed’ + the suffix -etum denoting an inhabitant.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Cheney II was born in 1520, in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England, his father, Robert Cheney Sr., was 31 and his mother, Annis Harrison, was 29. He married Joane Harrison before 4 January 1550, in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 31 October 1567, in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England, at the age of 47, and was buried in City of London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Cheney II est né en 1520, à Waltham Abbey, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Robert Cheney Sr., avait 31 ans et sa mère, Annis Harrison, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Joane Harrison avant 4 janvier 1550, à Waltham Abbey, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 31 octobre 1567, à Waltham Abbey, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à City of London, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Cheney IIが1520年にイギリスエセックスワルサム アビーで生まれた時，父Robert Cheney Sr.は31歳，母Annis Harrisonは29歳であった。1550年1月4日以前にイギリスエセックスワルサム アビーでJoane Harrisonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1567年10月31日にイギリスエセックスワルサム アビーで47歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシトイ オフ ロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Cheney II(이)가 1520년 영국 에세스 월섬 애브브이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Cheney Sr.(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Annis Harrison(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1550년 1월 4일 이전 영국 에세스 월섬 애브브이에서 Joane Harrison(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1567년 10월 31일 영국 에세스 월섬 애브브이에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 시티 프 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Cheney II nasceu em 1520, em Waltham Abbey, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Cheney Sr., tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Annis Harrison, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Joane Harrison antes 4 de janeiro de 1550, em Waltham Abbey, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 31 de outubro de 1567, em Waltham Abbey, Essex, Inglaterra, com 47 anos, e foi sepultado em City of London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Cheney II родился в 1520 г. в местности валтам эбби, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Cheney Sr. было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Annis Harrison, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Joane Harrison перед 4 январь 1550 г. в местности валтам эбби, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 31 октября 1567 г. в местности валтам эбби, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности сити оф лондон, Англия, Великобритания.
